title: Developer Portfolio
description: This website — a data-driven SPA built from scratch with vanilla JavaScript, Handlebars templating, and JSON data files. No build tools, no frameworks — just clean architecture for a static GitHub Pages site.
challenge: Building a portfolio that goes beyond a static HTML page — one that demonstrates actual software architecture skills while staying within the constraints of GitHub Pages static hosting. The site needed to be data-driven (content changes shouldn't require touching templates), support deep-linking to individual project case studies, and handle dark/light theming that respects the user's system preferences.
solution: I designed a data-driven SPA architecture where all content lives in JSON files, templates are compiled at runtime using Handlebars.js, and URL-param based routing enables project detail pages without a backend. The theme toggle uses localStorage for persistence and a MutationObserver to sync UI after async template rendering. The entire site is a demonstration of separation of concerns: data, templates, styling, and logic are each in their own layer.

features.title: SPA Architecture
features.desc: URL-param based routing with dynamic Handlebars template compilation and JSON data separation — no build step required.

features.title: System-Aware Theming
features.desc: Dark/light toggle that checks localStorage first, falls back to OS preference, and uses MutationObserver for render-safe UI sync.

features.title: Data-Driven Content
features.desc: All portfolio content stored in JSON files — adding a project means editing a JSON file, not rewriting HTML.

features.title: Fully Responsive
features.desc: Mobile-first layout with Bootstrap 5, collapsible navigation, and adaptive timeline components.
